structure_sections.script_template: How do <group> even <action> against <rivals> at a <event>?
structure_sections.target_fill: How do three high schoolers even compete against PhD teams from Stanford Harvard and MIT at a Harvard Hackathon
Well it starts with a 6 hour flight where Patrick watched anime the whole time and Jerry and I locked in researching

structure_sections.script_template: We <action> at <location> till <time>. Then we <action> at <time>, <actions>.
structure_sections.target_fill: We landed checked in and instead of sleeping we biked around Boston till like midnight Then we woke up at 7 got to Harvard sat through a couple talks and then started building something we thought was insane MedSim

a 3D trauma centered simulation on top of role models Where you upload a picture it builds a 3D version sends out agents and tells you what's inefficient and how to fix it After talking to a few judges we got some feedback
messed around a bit got an MVP working and did some dumb shit and then we biked to MassGen to get validation So while validating we realized this is not even close to good enough

structure_sections.script_template: So we're <action>. Right before <context> I see <reveal>.
structure_sections.target_fill: So we're like okay cool we're pulling an all nighter Right before we start I see some reel that says you lock in harder without a shirt

we chug a Monster and start coding shirts off Five hours later we have zero sleep completely fried and we actually have something decent One problem we're being judged on our pitch decks and we don't have one
So we sprint back to Harvard throw together slides on zero sleep and practice in the bathroom until we can say it without thinking We pitch
and at that point we're too tired to even care what happened until they start announcing winners
And then they call our name Jerry and I sprint up Patrick already left and somehow we pulled it off Three high schoolers

Most of it I think the biggest thing from this weekend is just how much the team matters None of that happens without the random dumb stuff The dance breaks the croissant eating just staying sane while everything is kind of falling apart Why season a week So yeah stay tuned

overlay_pattern.summary: This creator frequently employs picture-in-picture (PIP) videos, particularly in the early and middle sections of their reels, to enhance the narrative context. These animated overlays allow them to demonstrate concepts or provide commentary while maintaining viewer engagement. Static images and GIFs, although less frequent, are used effectively to punctuate moments or emphasize the creator's feelings at specific points in the narrative. Emoji graphics appear sparingly, serving as visual highlights in conjunction with spoken content.
